title: Mailchimp via Zapier
excerpt: I have two problems with the built-in integration with Mailchimp. It’s well-documented that nothing except the email address itself transfers over. However, I also expected monday.com to update Mailchimp if a subscriber …

posts.author: horosam (Samuel Horowitz)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have two problems with the built-in integration with Mailchimp. It’s well-documented that nothing except the email address itself transfers over. However, I also expected monday.com to update Mailchimp if a subscriber address changes and it does not. If I change an email address on my monday.com board, it only adds the new subscriber rather than upate.

So I read that a Zapier integration would be better. I’m having difficulties:

  1. I have a similar problem with the native integration: updates in monday.com result in new subscribers appearing. monday.com is not providing the previous email address for Mailchimp to use. (I have made a custom sync to mailchimp before and I am desperately trying to avoid that due to time constraints.)

  2. Even if I am willing to live with the lack of updates, I am not finding a way to map Status columns in monday.com to Groups (Interest Groups) in Mailchimp. Mailchimp’s built-in CSV importer can translate this just fine, so if I export my board and then import everything loads exactly as expected.

Is anybody willing to share how they’ve overcome these?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @horosam

We developed an app called Mailchimp Integration Plus that provides a robust integration with Mailchimp. Amongst other capabilities, it allows to do full mapping of fields from monday to Mailchimp, including custom fields for merge tags. Here’s an example →

Screenshot 2024-05-13 at 12.17.122714×1440 269 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!
